episode0.title: THE NAME — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2 UPGRADED

puzzle0.title: MEMORY RECONSTRUCTION
puzzle0.description: Use the recovered identity fragments to reconstruct the erased crew member.

event1.text: Operator, I found the access spine behind the relay console.

event3.text: It smells like hot plastic, old dust, and something I am choosing to call 'not blood' until proven otherwise.

event5.text: Noah said you already saw his name. If that is true, it is somewhere in the records we have already touched.

event7.text: The personnel memory index is three junctions ahead. I can reach it, but I need you calling the route.

event1.text: Main corridor. Fast route. Terrible personality.

event3.text: The floor plates are buckled. I am stepping over them, not on them.

event5.text: There is a maintenance drone wedged into the wall.

backgroundEvent10.text: I am moving. Fast, but not stupid-fast. I have learned from several stupid-fast mistakes.

event1.text: Service crawl. Narrow. Low ceiling. Deeply unfriendly to knees.

event3.text: I am going in sideways. If I get stuck, delete this from the official rescue transcript.

event5.text: There are old cable tags in here. Some are handwritten.

event7.text: That is already weird. Rivera hated handwriting on system labels.

backgroundEvent8.text: I found a tag marked VOSS. Not a file. Just a cable path.

choice1.text: IGNORE IT AND REACH THE INDEX.

event1.text: Drone casing is split. Someone forced it open from the outside.

event3.text: There is a cached route packet inside. That should not be stored in a maintenance drone.

event4.title: DRONE ROUTE CACHE
event4.content:
ROUTE CACHE / PARTIAL
DESTINATION: PERSONNEL MEMORY INDEX
RECENT QUERY FRAGMENT: ...VOSS...
OPERATOR: UNKNOWN
CACHE STATUS: DAMAGED
event4.caption: Recovered route packet from damaged maintenance drone.
event4.message: [DRONE ROUTE CACHE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: It runs toward the memory index, but it has been cut and spliced at least twice.

event5.text: Someone rerouted a person like a circuit.

event6.title: HANDWRITTEN CABLE TAG
event6.content:
HANDWRITTEN LABEL RECOVERED
TEXT: VOSS / MEMORY INDEX BYPASS
INK AGE: PRE-BLACKOUT
AUTHORIZED LABEL FORMAT: INVALID
event6.caption: A handwritten cable tag found inside the service crawl.
event6.message: [HANDWRITTEN CABLE TAG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event8.text: I hate how personal this is starting to feel.

event1.text: Moving to the index. No detours.

event3.text: The station keeps giving me little reasons to stop, which means stopping is either smart or exactly what it wants.

event5.text: I am choosing momentum. If I regret that, please pretend I looked brave.

event1.text: I am at the personnel memory index hatch.

event3.text: It is sealed manually. Someone wanted this shut even if the station came back online.

event5.text: I can force it, or we can scan the lock first.

event3.text: This is a bad idea. I am doing it anyway, which makes it an operational decision.

event8.text: Also sparked directly into my sleeve. I am fine. My sleeve is holding a different opinion.

event3.text: Good call. There is a tamper loop under the manual latch.

event5.text: If I had forced this without seeing it, it would have wiped the local cache.

event7.text: I am bypassing it now. Slowly. Calmly. Like a person who absolutely slept this week.

event3.text: Rows of memory cores. Most are dark. One is blinking like it is trying to get attention without looking desperate.

event4.title: CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT
event4.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
STATUS: ERASED
SURNAME: VOSS
MEMORY INDEX: DAMAGED
SOURCE CONFLICT: OFFICIAL MANIFEST DENIES ENTRY
event4.caption: Partially recovered crew record from the personnel memory index.
event4.message: [CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event8.text: I do not remember anyone named Voss on ECHO.

backgroundEvent9.text: YOU REMEMBER THE SHAPE OF THE HOLE.

event3.text: Before either of you accuse me of sounding ominous for sport, I know what that name can do in this system.

event5.text: Names are not just labels in ECHO's identity layer. They are keys.

event7.text: Some keys open doors. Some wake things that were shut down for a reason.

event1.text: There was a transfer request. Noah Voss. Communications specialist.

event3.text: Denied. Sealed. Filed under Omega clearance conflict, which is institutional language for 'do not ask while anyone important is listening.'

event7.text: I do not remember the answer. That fact is becoming less comforting by the minute.

event3.text: Hale can panic in complete sentences. I am going to make the dead computer talk.

event5.text: Also, Ethan, if this kills me, I am haunting your punctuation.

event7.text: No response. That means he either hated the joke or is worried. Possibly both.

event0.title: MAINTENANCE LOG
event0.content:
Relay inspection completed.
Signal bleed detected on emergency carrier.
Manual reset recommended.

Signed: N. Voss
event0.caption: Recovered relay maintenance entry with partial signature.

event4.text: The manifest gives us Voss. The maintenance log gives us N. Voss.

event6.text: Noah is starting to look less like a ghost story and more like a person someone removed very carefully.

choice1.text: TELL MAYA TO STEP BACK FIRST.

event3.text: The index is fighting me. Not blocking. Fighting. There is a difference.

event5.text: It keeps trying to redirect the query into an empty crew slot.

event3.text: I hate that I needed someone else to say that.

event5.text: My hands are shaking. Not dramatically. Just enough to make small buttons feel personally insulting.

event7.text: Okay. Breathing. Going back in slower.

event1.text: The scan needs a minute. I have to keep the core awake manually.

event3.text: If I let go, it drops the fragment and we start over.

event5.text: So I am standing here with one hand on a hot memory core, having a deeply normal day.

event0.text: Maya keeps the memory core active. The recovery is still running.

backgroundEvent1.text: Still here. Still attached to the world's angriest archive brick.

backgroundEvent2.text: I just remembered something stupid. Rivera used to hum when she debugged. I hated it. I would pay real money to hear it right now.

event3.text: We have the shape of him now. Not enough for a face. Enough for a name.

event4.title: IDENTITY FRAGMENT
event4.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
SURNAME: VOSS
INITIAL: N.
ROLE CLASS: COMMUNICATIONS
MEMORY INDEX STATE: SUPPRESSED
TRANSFER STATE: CONTRADICTORY
event4.caption: Recovered identity fragment assembled from memory index and maintenance records.

event6.text: If we enter the name, the system may restore the profile.

event8.text: Or it may let something answer to it.

event1.text: Give it exactly what the records support. Nothing poetic. Nothing guessed.

event3.text: The system will accept confidence it has not earned. That is how bad archives become worse gods.

event5.text: Name: Noah Voss. If you do this, do it cleanly.

event3.text: Wrong reconstruction. The archive snapped back hard.

event5.text: Don't improvise. N. Voss. Communications. Missing crew seven.

event7.text: We are not inventing a person. We are giving one back.

event3.text: Initial: N.

event5.text: Role class: communications. The recovered answer is Noah Voss.

event7.text: I wish saying that did not feel like turning a handle from the wrong side of a door.

event1.text: Okay. I will walk you through it.

event3.text: Noah Voss. Not just Voss. Not just Noah if the panel gets picky. Full name first.

event5.text: And if the archive screams, we pretend that is normal and keep our hands steady.

event1.title: NOAH VOSS
event1.content:
NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
CLEARANCE LEVEL: OMEGA
STATUS: UNKNOWN
TRANSFER STATUS: DENIED
MEMORY INDEX: SUPPRESSED
event1.caption: Reconstructed personnel profile from fragmented archive records.
event1.message: [NOAH VOSS PROFILE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: There is a second after the profile opens where nobody says anything.

event8.text: Because a deleted man just got his name back.

event3.text: Communications specialist. Of course he was.

event5.text: The first person erased from ECHO was the one whose job was to make sure people could speak.

event7.text: That is not irony. That is cruelty with paperwork.

event0.title: TRANSFER RECORD: NOAH VOSS
event0.content:
TRANSFER REQUEST: NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
STATUS: DENIED
DATE: 21 DAYS BEFORE LAUNCH
REASON: OMEGA CLEARANCE CONFLICT
FINAL NOTE: DO NOT ASSIGN TO ECHO
event0.caption: Recovered transfer record showing Noah Voss was officially denied station assignment.
event0.message: [NOAH VOSS TRANSFER RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event6.text: Then why was his name inside our station?

event8.text: Not where they let you remember me.

event10.text: I was the first voice you heard.

event12.text: Before Hale. Before Maya. Before the station learned how to pretend silence was safety.
